briefdescription.para: Get the CAN core clock rate.

detaileddescription.para: Returns the CAN core clock rate. One minimum time quantum (mtq) is 1/(core clock rate). The CAN core clock can be further divided by the CAN clock prescaler (see the can_timing struct), providing the time quantum (tq).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
CAN core clock rate in Hz.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or a negative error code on error

briefdescription.para: Get minimum supported bitrate.

detaileddescription.para: Get the minimum supported bitrate for the CAN controller/transceiver combination.

simplesect.para: The minimum bitrate represents limitations of the CAN controller/transceiver combination. Whether the CAN controller can achieve this bitrate depends on the CAN core clock rate and the minimum CAN timing limits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: Minimum supported bitrate in bits/s. A value of 0 means the lower limit is unspecified.

briefdescription.para: Get maximum supported bitrate.

detaileddescription.para: Get the maximum supported bitrate for the CAN controller/transceiver combination.

simplesect.para: The maximum bitrate represents limitations of the CAN controller/transceiver combination. Whether the CAN controller can achieve this bitrate depends on the CAN core clock rate and the maximum CAN timing limits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: Maximum supported bitrate in bits/s

briefdescription.para: Get the minimum supported timing parameter values.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the minimum supported timing parameter values.

briefdescription.para: Get the maximum supported timing parameter values.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the maximum supported timing parameter values.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct can_timing *res, uint32_t bitrate, uint16_t sample_pnt)
memberdef.name: can_calc_timing

briefdescription.para: Calculate timing parameters from bitrate and sample point.

detaileddescription.para: Calculate the timing parameters from a given bitrate in bits/s and the sampling point in permill (1/1000) of the entire bit time. The bitrate must always match perfectly. If no result can be reached for the given parameters, -EINVAL is returned.
If the sample point is set to 0, this function defaults to a sample point of 75.0% for bitrates over 800 kbit/s, 80.0% for bitrates over 500 kbit/s, and 87.5% for all other bitrates.

simplesect.para: The requested sample_pnt will not always be matched perfectly. The algorithm calculates the best possible match.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Result is written into the can_timing struct provided.
Target bitrate in bits/s.
Sample point in permille of the entire bit time or 0 for automatic sample point location.
or positive sample point error on success.
if the requested bitrate or sample point is out of range.
if the requested bitrate is not supported.
if can_get_core_clock() is not available.

briefdescription.para: Get the minimum supported timing parameter values for the data phase.

detaileddescription.para: Same as can_get_timing_min() but for the minimum values for the data phase.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_FD_MODE must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the minimum supported timing parameter values, or NULL if CAN FD support is not implemented by the driver.

briefdescription.para: Get the maximum supported timing parameter values for the data phase.

detaileddescription.para: Same as can_get_timing_max() but for the maximum values for the data phase.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_FD_MODE must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the maximum supported timing parameter values, or NULL if CAN FD support is not implemented by the driver.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct can_timing *res, uint32_t bitrate, uint16_t sample_pnt)
memberdef.name: can_calc_timing_data

briefdescription.para: Calculate timing parameters for the data phase.

detaileddescription.para: Same as can_calc_timing() but with the maximum and minimum values from the data phase.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_FD_MODE must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Result is written into the can_timing struct provided.
Target bitrate for the data phase in bits/s
Sample point for the data phase in permille of the entire bit time or 0 for automatic sample point location.

simplesect.para: 0 or positive sample point error on success.

parameterdescription.para: if the requested bitrate or sample point is out of range.
if the requested bitrate is not supported.
if can_get_core_clock() is not available.

briefdescription.para: Configure the bus timing for the data phase of a CAN FD controller.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_FD_MODE must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Bus timings for data phase
If successful.
if the CAN controller is not in stopped state.
General input/output error, failed to configure device.
if the timing parameters are not supported by the driver.
if CAN FD support is not implemented by the driver.

briefdescription.para: Set the bitrate for the data phase of the CAN FD controller.

detaileddescription.para: CAN in Automation (CiA) 301 v4.2.0 recommends a sample point location of 87.5% percent for all bitrates. However, some CAN controllers have difficulties meeting this for higher bitrates.

This function defaults to using a sample point of 75.0% for bitrates over 800 kbit/s, 80.0% for bitrates over 500 kbit/s, and 87.5% for all other bitrates. This is in line with the sample point locations used by the Linux kernel.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_FD_MODE must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Desired data phase bitrate.
If successful.
if the CAN controller is not in stopped state.
if the requested bitrate is out of range.
if the requested bitrate not supported by the CAN controller/transceiver combination.
if the resulting sample point is off by more than +/- 5%.
General input/output error, failed to set bitrate.

briefdescription.para: Configure the bus timing of a CAN controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Bus timings.
If successful.
if the CAN controller is not in stopped state.
if the timing parameters are not supported by the driver.
General input/output error, failed to configure device.

briefdescription.para: Get the supported modes of the CAN controller.

detaileddescription.para: The returned capabilities may not necessarily be supported at the same time (e.g. some CAN controllers support both CAN_MODE_LOOPBACK and CAN_MODE_LISTENONLY, but not at the same time).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Supported capabilities.
If successful.
General input/output error, failed to get capabilities.

briefdescription.para: Get the CAN transceiver associated with the CAN controller.

detaileddescription.para: Get a pointer to the device structure for the CAN transceiver associated with the CAN controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the device structure for the associated CAN transceiver driver instance, or NULL if no transceiver is associated.

briefdescription.para: Start the CAN controller.

detaileddescription.para: Bring the CAN controller out of CAN_STATE_STOPPED. This will reset the RX/TX error counters, enable the CAN controller to participate in CAN communication, and enable the CAN transceiver, if supported.
Starting the CAN controller resets all the CAN controller statistics.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
if successful.
if the device is already started.
General input/output error, failed to start device.

briefdescription.para: Stop the CAN controller.

detaileddescription.para: Bring the CAN controller into CAN_STATE_STOPPED. This will disallow the CAN controller from participating in CAN communication, abort any pending CAN frame transmissions, and disable the CAN transceiver, if supported.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
if successful.
if the device is already stopped.
General input/output error, failed to stop device.

briefdescription.para: Set the CAN controller to the given operation mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Operation mode.
If successful.
if the CAN controller is not in stopped state.
General input/output error, failed to configure device.

briefdescription.para: Get the operation mode of the CAN controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: Current operation mode.

briefdescription.para: Set the bitrate of the CAN controller.

detaileddescription.para: CAN in Automation (CiA) 301 v4.2.0 recommends a sample point location of 87.5% percent for all bitrates. However, some CAN controllers have difficulties meeting this for higher bitrates.

This function defaults to using a sample point of 75.0% for bitrates over 800 kbit/s, 80.0% for bitrates over 500 kbit/s, and 87.5% for all other bitrates. This is in line with the sample point locations used by the Linux kernel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Desired arbitration phase bitrate.
If successful.
if the CAN controller is not in stopped state.
if the requested bitrate is out of range.
if the requested bitrate not supported by the CAN controller/transceiver combination.
if the resulting sample point is off by more than +/- 5%.
General input/output error, failed to set bitrate.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct can_frame *frame, k_timeout_t timeout, can_tx_callback_t callback, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: can_send

briefdescription.para: Queue a CAN frame for transmission on the CAN bus.

detaileddescription.para: Queue a CAN frame for transmission on the CAN bus with optional timeout and completion callback function.

Queued CAN frames are transmitted in order according to the their priority:
The lower the CAN-ID, the higher the priority.
Data frames have higher priority than Remote Transmission Request (RTR) frames with identical CAN-IDs.
Frames with standard (11-bit) identifiers have higher priority than frames with extended (29-bit) identifiers with identical base IDs (the higher 11 bits of the extended identifier).
Transmission order for queued frames with the same priority is hardware dependent.

If transmitting segmented messages spanning multiple CAN frames with identical CAN-IDs, the sender must ensure to only queue one frame at a time if FIFO order is required.
By default, the CAN controller will automatically retry transmission in case of lost bus arbitration or missing acknowledge. Some CAN controllers support disabling automatic retransmissions via CAN_MODE_ONE_SHOT.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
CAN frame to transmit.
Timeout waiting for a empty TX mailbox or K_FOREVER.
Optional callback for when the frame was sent or a transmission error occurred. If NULL, this function is blocking until frame is sent. The callback must be NULL if called from user mode.
User data to pass to callback function.
if successful.
if an invalid parameter was passed to the function.
if an unsupported parameter was passed to the function.
if the CAN controller is in stopped state.
if the CAN controller is in bus-off state.
if CAN bus arbitration was lost (only applicable if automatic retransmissions are disabled).
if a general transmit error occurred (e.g. missing ACK if automatic retransmissions are disabled).
on timeout.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, can_rx_callback_t callback, void *user_data, const struct can_filter *filter)
memberdef.name: can_add_rx_filter

briefdescription.para: Add a callback function for a given CAN filter.

detaileddescription.para: Add a callback to CAN identifiers specified by a filter. When a received CAN frame matching the filter is received by the CAN controller, the callback function is called in interrupt context.
If a received frame matches more than one filter (i.e., the filter IDs/masks or flags overlap), the priority of the match is hardware dependent.
The same callback function can be used for multiple filters.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
This function is called by the CAN controller driver whenever a frame matching the filter is received.
User data to pass to callback function.
Pointer to a can_filter structure defining the filter.
on success.
if there are no free filters.
if the requested filter type is invalid.
if the requested filter type is not supported.
General input/output error, failed to add filter.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct k_msgq *msgq, const struct can_filter *filter)
memberdef.name: can_add_rx_filter_msgq

briefdescription.para: Simple wrapper function for adding a message queue for a given filter.

detaileddescription.para: Wrapper function for can_add_rx_filter() which puts received CAN frames matching the filter in a message queue instead of calling a callback.
If a received frame matches more than one filter (i.e., the filter IDs/masks or flags overlap), the priority of the match is hardware dependent.
The same message queue can be used for multiple filters.

simplesect.para: The message queue must be initialized before calling this function and the caller must have appropriate permissions on it.
Message queue overruns are silently ignored and overrun frames discarded. Custom error handling can be implemented by using can_add_rx_filter() and k_msgq_put() directly.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to the already initialized k_msgq struct.
Pointer to a can_filter structure defining the filter.
on success.
if there are no free filters.
if the requested filter type is invalid.
if the requested filter type is not supported.
General input/output error, failed to add filter.

briefdescription.para: Remove a CAN RX filter.

detaileddescription.para: This routine removes a CAN RX filter based on the filter ID returned by can_add_rx_filter() or can_add_rx_filter_msgq().

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Filter ID

briefdescription.para: Get maximum number of RX filters.

detaileddescription.para: Get the maximum number of concurrent RX filters for the CAN controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Get the maximum standard (11-bit) CAN ID filters if false, or extended (29-bit) CAN ID filters if true.
number of maximum concurrent filters.
General input/output error.
If this function is not implemented by the driver.

briefdescription.para: Statically define and initialize a CAN RX message queue.

detaileddescription.para: The message queue's ring buffer contains space for max_frames CAN frames.

parameterdescription.para: Name of the message queue.
Maximum number of CAN frames that can be queued.

sectiondef.header: CAN bus error reporting and handling

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum can_state *state, struct can_bus_err_cnt *err_cnt)
memberdef.name: can_get_state

briefdescription.para: Get current CAN controller state.

detaileddescription.para: Returns the current state and optionally the error counter values of the CAN controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to the state destination enum or NULL.
Pointer to the err_cnt destination structure or NULL.
If successful.
General input/output error, failed to get state.

briefdescription.para: Recover from bus-off state.

detaileddescription.para: Recover the CAN controller from bus-off state to error-active state.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_MANUAL_RECOVERY_MODE must be enabled for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Timeout for waiting for the recovery or K_FOREVER.
on success.
if the CAN controller is not in manual recovery mode.
if the CAN controller is in stopped state.
on timeout.
If this function is not implemented by the driver.
General input/output error, failed to recover.

briefdescription.para: Set a callback for CAN controller state change events.

detaileddescription.para: Set the callback for CAN controller state change events. The callback function will be called in interrupt context.
Only one callback can be registered per controller. Calling this function again overrides any previously registered callback.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Callback function.
User data to pass to callback function.

briefdescription.para: Get the bit error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The bit error counter is incremented when the CAN controller is unable to transmit either a dominant or a recessive bit.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_STATS must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: bit error counter

briefdescription.para: Get the bit0 error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The bit0 error counter is incremented when the CAN controller is unable to transmit a dominant bit.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_STATS must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: bit0 error counter

briefdescription.para: Get the bit1 error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The bit1 error counter is incremented when the CAN controller is unable to transmit a recessive bit.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_STATS must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: bit1 error counter

briefdescription.para: Get the stuffing error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The stuffing error counter is incremented when the CAN controller detects a bit stuffing error.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_STATS must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: stuffing error counter

briefdescription.para: Get the CRC error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The CRC error counter is incremented when the CAN controller detects a frame with an invalid CRC.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_STATS must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: CRC error counter

briefdescription.para: Get the form error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The form error counter is incremented when the CAN controller detects a fixed-form bit field containing illegal bits.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_STATS must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: form error counter

briefdescription.para: Get the acknowledge error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The acknowledge error counter is incremented when the CAN controller does not monitor a dominant bit in the ACK slot.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_STATS must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: acknowledge error counter

briefdescription.para: Get the RX overrun counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The RX overrun counter is incremented when the CAN controller receives a CAN frame matching an installed filter but lacks the capacity to store it (either due to an already full RX mailbox or a full RX FIFO).

simplesect.para: CONFIG_CAN_STATS must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: RX overrun counter

briefdescription.para: Convert from Data Length Code (DLC) to the number of data bytes.

parameterdescription.para: Data Length Code (DLC).

simplesect.para: Number of bytes.

briefdescription.para: Convert from number of bytes to Data Length Code (DLC)

parameterdescription.para: Number of bytes.

simplesect.para: Data Length Code (DLC).

briefdescription.para: Check if a CAN frame matches a CAN filter.

parameterdescription.para: CAN frame.
CAN filter.

simplesect.para: true if the CAN frame matches the CAN filter, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Bit mask for a standard (11-bit) CAN identifier.
Bit mask for an extended (29-bit) CAN identifier.
Maximum data length code for CAN 2.0A/2.0B.
Maximum data length code for CAN FD.
Normal mode.
Controller is in loopback mode (receives own frames).
Controller is not allowed to send dominant bits.
Controller allows transmitting/receiving CAN FD frames.
Controller does not retransmit in case of lost arbitration or missing ACK.
Controller uses triple sampling mode.
Controller requires manual recovery after entering bus-off state.
Frame uses extended (29-bit) CAN ID.
Frame is a Remote Transmission Request (RTR)
Frame uses CAN FD format (FDF)
Frame uses CAN FD Baud Rate Switch (BRS).

detaileddescription.para: Only valid in combination with CAN_FRAME_FDF.

briefdescription.para: CAN FD Error State Indicator (ESI).

detaileddescription.para: Indicates that the transmitting node is in error-passive state. Only valid in combination with CAN_FRAME_FDF.

briefdescription.para: Filter matches frames with extended (29-bit) CAN IDs.
Error-active state (RX/TX error count < 96).
Error-warning state (RX/TX error count < 128).
Error-passive state (RX/TX error count < 256).
Bus-off state (RX/TX error count >= 256).
CAN controller is stopped and does not participate in CAN communication.
Defines the state of the CAN controller.
Provides a type to hold CAN controller configuration flags.

detaileddescription.para: The lower 24 bits are reserved for common CAN controller mode flags. The upper 8 bits are reserved for CAN controller/driver specific flags.

simplesect.para: CAN_MODE_FLAGS.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* can_tx_callback_t) (const struct device *dev, int error, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: can_tx_callback_t

briefdescription.para: Defines the application callback handler function signature.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Status of the performed send operation. See the list of return values for can_send() for value descriptions.
User data provided when the frame was sent.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* can_rx_callback_t) (const struct device *dev, struct can_frame *frame, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: can_rx_callback_t

briefdescription.para: Defines the application callback handler function signature for receiving.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Received frame.
User data provided when the filter was added.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* can_state_change_callback_t) (const struct device *dev, enum can_state state, struct can_bus_err_cnt err_cnt, void *user_data)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, enum can_state state, struct can_bus_err_cnt err_cnt, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: can_state_change_callback_t

briefdescription.para: Defines the state change callback handler function signature.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
State of the CAN controller.
CAN controller error counter values.
User data provided the callback was set.

briefdescription.para: Increment the bit error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The bit error counter is incremented when the CAN controller is unable to transmit either a dominant or a recessive bit.

simplesect.para: This error counter should only be incremented if the CAN controller is unable to distinguish between failure to transmit a dominant versus failure to transmit a recessive bit. If the CAN controller supports distinguishing between the two, the bit0 or bit1 error counter shall be incremented instead.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Increment the bit0 error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The bit0 error counter is incremented when the CAN controller is unable to transmit a dominant bit.

Incrementing this counter will automatically increment the bit error counter.
CAN_STATS_BIT_ERROR_INC()
dev_
Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Increment the bit1 (recessive) error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The bit1 error counter is incremented when the CAN controller is unable to transmit a recessive bit.

Incrementing this counter will automatically increment the bit error counter.
CAN_STATS_BIT_ERROR_INC()
dev_
Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Increment the stuffing error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The stuffing error counter is incremented when the CAN controller detects a bit stuffing error.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Increment the CRC error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The CRC error counter is incremented when the CAN controller detects a frame with an invalid CRC.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Increment the form error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The form error counter is incremented when the CAN controller detects a fixed-form bit field containing illegal bits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Increment the acknowledge error counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The acknowledge error counter is incremented when the CAN controller does not monitor a dominant bit in the ACK slot.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Increment the RX overrun counter for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The RX overrun counter is incremented when the CAN controller receives a CAN frame matching an installed filter but lacks the capacity to store it (either due to an already full RX mailbox or a full RX FIFO).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Zero all statistics for a CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: The driver is responsible for resetting the statistics before starting the CAN controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

memberdef.name: CAN_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: Z_CAN_DEVICE_STATE_DEFINE(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id)); \ Z_CAN_INIT_FN(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), init_fn) \ Z_DEVICE_DEFINE(node_id, Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), \ DEVICE_DT_NAME(node_id), \ &UTIL_CAT(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), _init), \ NULL, Z_DEVICE_DT_FLAGS(node_id), pm, data, \ config, level, prio, api, \ &(Z_DEVICE_STATE_NAME(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id)).devstate), \ __VA_ARGS__)

briefdescription.para: Like DEVICE_DT_DEFINE() with CAN device specifics.

detaileddescription.para: Defines a device which implements the CAN API. May generate a custom device_state container struct and init_fn wrapper when needed depending on CONFIG_CAN_STATS.

parameterdescription.para: The devicetree node identifier.
Name of the init function of the driver.
PM device resources reference (NULL if device does not use PM).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Priority within the selected initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.

briefdescription.para: Like CAN_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE() for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.

parameterdescription.para: Instance number. This is replaced by DT_DRV_COMPAT(inst) in the call to CAN_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE().
Other parameters as expected by CAN_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE().

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for CAN controllers.
